info.title: 10-8 Systems — Incident Export Webhook
info.description:
Real-time webhook export of incidents/calls for service from 10-8 Systems.

Events are triggered when calls are **created**, **modified**, **closed**, or **reopened**.
10-8 Systems sends an HTTP `POST` to the subscriber's configured endpoint with the payload
described below. The subscriber must respond with a `200` status code to acknowledge receipt.

## Delivery Guarantees

Events are delivered via a **FIFO (first-in, first-out) queue**, preserving the original
order in which incidents were created or modified.

If the subscriber endpoint does not return a `200` response, the event remains in the queue
and delivery is retried. Events that continue to fail are held in the queue for **up to 2 days**,
after which they are purged. While events are pending or being retried, subsequent events for
the same subscriber remain queued behind them to preserve order.

post.summary: Incident created, updated, closed, or reopened
post.description:
Fired whenever an incident is created, modified, closed, or reopened.
The full incident object is included on every event.

200.description:
Event acknowledged by subscriber. Any non-200 response causes the event to be
retried from the FIFO queue for up to 2 days before being purged.

action.description: The action that triggered the event.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp of when incident was created.

dispatcher.description: Name of user who created incident.

units.description: Array of unit objects assigned to the incident.

Case.description: A case number assigned to the incident.

id.description: Internal 10-8 Systems ID for case number.

createdBy.description: Internal 10-8 Systems ID of user that generated case number.

IncidentID.description: A responding agency's incident/call ID. Only some agencies will have this configured.

id.description: Internal 10-8 Systems ID for agency incident ID.

agency.description: Name of agency to which this incident ID is assigned.

Person.description: A person involved in the incident.

relation.description: Relation of person to the incident (e.g. suspect, witness).

Vehicle.description: A vehicle involved in the incident.

user.description: Name of user who created comment.

time.description: Local timestamp string of when comment was added.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp of when comment was added.

Unit.description: A unit/responder assigned to the incident.

unit.description: Name of unit/responder assigned to incident.

department.description: Name of department/agency the unit is assigned to.

run_number.description: Formatted string of response run number.

dispatched.description: Local timestamp of when unit was assigned to the incident.

enroute.description: Local timestamp of when unit was placed in en route status.

time_enroute.description: Unix timestamp of en route time.

onscene.description: Local timestamp of when unit arrived on scene.

time_onscene.description: Unix timestamp of on scene time.

transporting.description: Local timestamp of when unit began transporting/en route to secondary location.

destination.description: Local timestamp of when unit arrived at destination/secondary location.

complete.description: Local timestamp of when unit was cleared from the incident.

destination_name.description: Name of destination/secondary location unit transported to.

Personnel.description: A user/person assigned to a responding unit.

Disposition.description: A disposition set by the user when the incident was closed.

timestamp.description: Unix timestamp of when disposition was submitted.

StructureFireUpdate.summary: Structure fire — update event with multiple responding units

disposition.notes: Patrol checked area and unable to locate.
